titleStmt.title: Letter from Fr Michael Saul SJ to Fr Thomas V Nolan SJ, 31 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael Saul

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Irish Jesuit Michael Saul (1884-1932) to his Father Provincial Thomas V Nolan SJ (1867-1941) in which he refers to a letter he wrote to the editor of An Claidheamh Soluis complaining 'how foolish people are who write and squabble in English in his paper to promote Irish!'
Born in Drumconrath village (Co. Meath) in 1884, Michael Saul organised a branch of the Gaelic League in Drumconrath in 1904 before he joined the Jesuits in 1909. It was remarked that "he never lost his early love and zeal for Irish…. few men have done more and laboured more for our language without notoriety or self- advertisement…”. Ordained in 1919, he was a member of An Fáinne, and the Coiste Gnótha of the Gaelic League. From 1922-27 he was attached to Belvedere College and was also assistant Director of An Timire (the Irish language Messenger). From 1927-30, he ministered in St Francis Xavier's, Upper Gardiner St., Dublin. In 1930 he was posted to Irish Jesuit mission to Hong Kong and in 1931 he went to the Catholic Mission in Canton, China. Here he contacted cholera and died on June 21st 1932. He is buried in Canton.

correspAction.placeName: St Francis Xavier's, Upper Gardiner Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mungret College,
Limerick 31.7.16
Very Rev. Fr. Provincial
P.C. I wrote a letter
to the Claidheamh Soluis about Irish in
general and how foolish people are
who write and squabble in English
in his paper to promote Irish! I sent it to the Rector who said he
sent it out to you, but then found he
had enclosed my letter to him by
mistake and tore the real letter in two.
He sent it to me telling me to rewrite
it and forward it as soon as
possible. I put down a nom
de plume but he crossed it out and
put down my name.
Of course it is all the same to me,
name or not: I got ideas, had
practice in writing, tried to do
something for the cause, and so
rest content. Very many thanks for being so kind
as to send me to Theology, which
news I heard this morning.
The address of the Claidheamh Soluis is: —
Fear eagarthóir an Claidheamh Soluis
40 Ché Urumhan Íochtarach
baile atha cliath
Editor of C...
40 Ormond quad Lower
Dublin Yours most obediently in Christ,
M Saul S.J.
